page:
# Day 2: Ocean Geography
## Focus
Ocean basins, tectonic activity, and how scientists map the seafloor.
## Learning Objectives
- Locate major ocean basins and prominent seafloor features. - Explain how plate tectonics shape marine environments. - Interpret why bathymetry matters for circulation, habitat, and exploration.
## Core Topics
### Ocean Basins and Features
Review continental shelves, slopes, abyssal plains, trenches, seamounts, and mid-ocean ridges.
### Plate Tectonics
Oceanic crust is created at spreading centers and recycled at subduction zones. These processes influence volcanic activity, trench formation, hydrothermal systems, and habitat distribution.
### Bathymetry and Sonar
Scientists use sonar and remote sensing to build maps of the seafloor. Bathymetric data helps researchers understand topography, currents, and access to different marine regions.
## Applied Activity
Create a labeled sketch of a cross-section from coastline to trench. Include:
- Continental shelf - Continental slope - Abyssal plain - Mid-ocean ridge or trench
## Reflection Prompt
How does seafloor structure shape the kinds of ecosystems that can exist there?
